visual_use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.

cards.title: Chanlun Buy Sell Points Reference
cards.desc: Abstract comparison card for first, second, and third buy/sell points. Use only with level, structure basis, and invalidation.
text_labels: This card shows relative positions. The actual level must be named before use.
Buy side: divergence, retest, third-buy pullback
Sell side: top divergence, rebound, third-sell pullback
cards.use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.
cards.use_for: relative positions of first, second, and third buy/sell points
cards.do_not_use_for: declaring a buy/sell point without level, structure basis, and invalidation

cards.title: Chanlun Center ZG ZD Reference
cards.desc: Abstract comparison card for center overlap, ZG/ZD, extension, and upgrade. Exact measurement requires OHLC data.
text_labels: A center is the overlapping zone of at least three consecutive same-level moves.
If ZD <= ZG, the three moves overlap and define a center.
After repeated extensions, reassess whether a larger-level center is forming.
cards.use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.
cards.use_for: center overlap, ZG/ZD, extension, and upgrade

cards.title: Chanlun Divergence MACD Reference
cards.desc: Abstract comparison card for divergence and MACD auxiliary force comparison. MACD is not the primary Chanlun rule.
text_labels: MACD helps compare force. It does not replace center/trend qualification.
Only valid if same-level trend conditions are met.
cards.use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.
cards.use_for: divergence shape plus MACD auxiliary force comparison
cards.do_not_use_for: treating MACD as the primary Chanlun rule

cards.title: Chanlun Fractal And Stroke Reference
cards.desc: Abstract comparison card for fractals, inclusion normalization, and strokes. Confirm from OHLC sequence, not from this drawing alone.
text_labels: Use as an abstract comparison card. Confirm from OHLC sequence, not from this drawing alone.
middle bar has highest high and higher low
middle bar has lowest low and lower high
merge contained bars before fractal detection
Valid stroke: opposite fractals with enough processed bars
Practical guard: if there are fewer than 5 processed K bars, do not claim a confirmed stroke.
cards.use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.
cards.use_for: fractal, inclusion processing, and stroke confirmation
cards.do_not_use_for: automatic K-line recognition or stroke confirmation without enough processed bars

cards.title: Chanlun Interval Nesting Reference
cards.desc: Abstract comparison card for large-level direction and small-level timing. Do not mix unrelated levels.
text_labels: Large level sets direction; small level times entry.
cards.use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.
cards.do_not_use_for: mixing unrelated levels or overriding a larger-level sell point

cards.title: Chanlun Visual Card Overview
cards.desc: Portable abstract comparison card for agent-stock Chanlun analysis. Use with rules.md; not for automatic chart recognition.
text_labels: Portable abstract cards for structure comparison. Use with ../rules.md and do not treat as automatic chart recognition.
opposite fractals plus enough processed bars
three same-level moves overlap: ZD <= ZG
buy point type must include level and invalidation
MACD area is auxiliary, not the primary rule
large level direction, small level timing
1. name level -> 2. normalize bars -> 3. find fractals/strokes/segments -> 4. locate center -> 5. check divergence/buy-sell point -> 6. fuse money/sector/news -> 7. output invalidation.
If data is summarized or insufficient, lower confidence and state what cannot be confirmed.
cards.use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.
cards.use_for: one-page routing map for all Chanlun visual cards

cards.title: Chanlun Segment Break Reference
cards.desc: Abstract comparison card for segment composition and break reasoning. Do not use as a simple price breakout rule.
text_labels: A segment needs at least three strokes. A break needs valid opposite sequence evidence.
Segment formed by at least 3 strokes
Break: new opposite sequence confirms old segment ended
Do not treat a simple price poke as a segment break.
Check whether the characteristic sequence belongs to the same opposite structure.
cards.use_boundary: Use SVG cards as qualitative shape references only. Do not use them for automatic K-line recognition, exact measurement, or buy/sell point declaration without level and invalidation.
cards.do_not_use_for: simple price breakout judgment without characteristic-sequence reasoning
